insights: Facsimile/printed signatures rejected 14% (2/14); confirmed listings feature authentic on-card autos with 80%+ delta.
Novelty cards and reprint flags appear exclusively in rejections; zero overlap with confirmed genuine vintage.
Gold-leafed/special edition signature products (Merrick Mint, Gold Edition) rejected; confirms real snipes are standard retail releases.
Wrong player/set/number issues account for 21% of rejections (3/14); confirmed listings have exact title-to-card match.
Multi-player or incomplete set listings rejected 100%; confirmed snipes are single-card, complete items.
Emoji-heavy titles with ALL CAPS modifiers (INVESTMENT, RARE, MINT) correlate with rejection; confirmed use neutral formal language.
Market price mismatches and 'other' category represent 36% of rejections; confirmed items show 80%+ underpricing vs. market average.
Confirmed snipes: authenticated vintage autos in standard product (2001 Authentic Images, 2023 Topps Tribute) priced 81–82% below market.
